title: Listening - Part 2
description: You will hear an archaeologist called Elena Rossi talking about her discovery of an ancient settlement in the Amazon rainforest. For questions 7 – 14, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.
instructions: You will hear an archaeologist called Elena Rossi talking about her discovery of an ancient settlement in the Amazon rainforest. For questions 7 – 14, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

extracts.context: Elena Rossi discusses the challenges and triumphs of her latest archaeological expedition.
extracts.audio_script: Good evening. My name is Elena Rossi and I've spent the last decade exploring the dense canopy of the Amazon. Many people think archaeology is all about gold and treasure, but for me, it's about understanding human resilience. [7]I first became interested in this specific region after reading a forgotten diary in a local library[/7]. The author described a 'city of stone' that no one had seen for centuries. Finding the site was an ordeal. The humidity was oppressive, and [8]I found that the constant presence of biting insects was the most taxing part of the journey[/8], even more so than the physical exhaustion. We used LiDAR technology to map the ground from the air, which revealed structures hidden beneath the trees. [9]The discovery of a large stone amphitheatre was the moment we realized the scale of the settlement[/9]. It suggested a highly organized society. One of the most interesting aspects was their water management. [10]They had constructed an intricate network of canals to prevent flooding during the monsoon season[/10]. As for our camp, it was basic. We slept in hammocks, and [11]I was surprised to find that the lack of privacy was actually quite difficult to handle[/11] over several months. Despite the hardships, the local community was incredibly supportive. [12]Learning about their traditional medicinal plants was an enlightening experience for the whole team[/12]. We are now working on preserving the site. [13]I am convinced that climate change is the biggest threat to these ruins[/13] because of the changing rainfall patterns. As we often say in the field, [14]we are not just digging up dirt, we are 'uncovering stories' that have been silenced for too long[/14].

dialogue.speaker: elena
dialogue.text: Good evening. My name is Elena Rossi and I've spent the last decade exploring the dense canopy of the Amazon. Many people think archaeology is all about gold and treasure, but for me, it's about understanding human resilience. I first became interested in this specific region after reading a forgotten diary in a local library. The author described a 'city of stone' that no one had seen for centuries. Finding the site was an ordeal. The humidity was oppressive, and I found that the constant presence of biting insects was the most taxing part of the journey, even more so than the physical exhaustion. We used LiDAR technology to map the ground from the air, which revealed structures hidden beneath the trees. The discovery of a large stone amphitheatre was the moment we realized the scale of the settlement. It suggested a highly organized society. One of the most interesting aspects was their water management. They had constructed an intricate network of canals to prevent flooding during the monsoon season. As for our camp, it was basic. We slept in hammocks, and I was surprised to find that the lack of privacy was actually quite difficult to handle over several months. Despite the hardships, the local community was incredibly supportive. Learning about their traditional medicinal plants was an enlightening experience for the whole team. We are now working on preserving the site. I am convinced that climate change is the biggest threat to these ruins because of the changing rainfall patterns. As we often say in the field, we are not just digging up dirt, we are 'uncovering stories' that have been silenced for too long.

questions.explanation: Finding the stone amphitheatre made them realize the scale of the settlement.

questions.explanation: They constructed an intricate network of canals to prevent flooding.

questions.explanation: She was surprised that the lack of privacy was difficult to handle.

questions.explanation: She notes that learning about medicinal plants was enlightening.

questions.explanation: She explicitly states climate change is the biggest threat.

questions.explanation: She says they are 'uncovering stories' that have been silenced.
